panels.visual_description: Close-up, High Angle. Professor Aronnax is seated at a desk, head in hands. The style is strictly Botanical Illustration: thin, precise ink lines define his features, with flat, pale watercolor washes. Large drops of perspiration on his forehead are drawn with crystalline clarity, resembling dew on a leaf. The background suggests the metal of the Nautilus but is rendered with organic, vine-like piping.
panels.dialogue: To hunt the tiger in the jungle... one might laugh. But the shark in its natural element...

panels.visual_description: A visualization of Aronnax's thought. A vintage biological plate illustration of a shark, rendered in desaturated sepia and grey. The shark is static but terrifyingly detailed, surrounded by scientific labels. The composition is melancholic and cold, emphasizing the void of the ocean.
panels.dialogue: I passed my hand over my forehead. Cold perspiration. Going up and down at the bottom of the sea... where one is almost certain to meet them.

panels.visual_description: Mid-shot. Aronnax looks up, eyes wide and hollow. The lighting is soft and diffuse, typical of botanical art prints. He looks at his own hands as if they are foreign objects.
panels.dialogue: I am not a negro from the Andamans. A little hesitation would not be ill-timed.

panels.visual_description: Medium Long Shot. Ned Land and Conseil enter the room. They are drawn with crisp outlines and warmer, flat colors (ochre and muted red) contrasting with the blue-grey gloom of the room. Their expressions are naive and joyous, framed by the arched doorway like a portrait.
panels.dialogue: Faith, sir! Your Captain Nemo—the devil take him!—has just made us a very pleasant offer.

panels.visual_description: Over-the-shoulder view from behind Aronnax. Ned leans in excitedly. The background features a large porthole showing the deep sea, drawn with flat layers of blue.
panels.dialogue: If agreeable to you, sir, the commander has invited us to visit the magnificent Ceylon fisheries to-morrow.

panels.visual_description: Close-up on Aronnax's face, shadowed. The style emphasizes the heavy linework around his eyes to show fatigue and dread (Melancholic tone). He forces a neutral expression.
panels.dialogue: He said nothing more? Nothing about... sharks?

panels.visual_description: Two-shot. Conseil arranges equipment, oblivious. Ned sits on an ottoman. The furniture is drawn with the intricate detail of a floral engraving.
panels.dialogue: Nothing more, sir. Just a little walk. But tell us, sir, what is a pearl?

panels.visual_description: Wide Shot. The three men sitting in the salon. The scene is framed by large underwater plants visible through the window, drawn in exquisite botanical detail. The interior feels like a quiet museum exhibit.
panels.dialogue: My worthy Ned, to the poet, a pearl is a tear of the sea; to the chemist, phosphate and carbonate of lime.

panels.visual_description: Insert Shot / Macro. A stylized, cross-section scientific diagram of an oyster ('Meleagrina margaritifera'). Flat colors show the layers of nacre forming around a grain of sand. The lines are extremely fine, resembling a 19th-century lithograph.
panels.dialogue: It is simply a morbid secretion. A grain of sand intrudes, and the oyster covers it with pearly matter, year after year.

panels.visual_description: Montage / Split focus. Various shells (mussels, earshells) float across the panel against a parchment-colored background, labeled with elegant handwriting. Aronnax's hand points to them.
panels.dialogue: Mussels in Scotland, France, and Ireland can do it too. But the precious pintadine is the true pearl-oyster.

panels.visual_description: Medium Shot. Aronnax is standing, gesturing. The atmosphere is calm, but his shadow on the wall is elongated and jagged, hinting at his internal state.
panels.dialogue: Some oysters are a perfect casket. One was mentioned as having contained no less than a hundred and fifty sharks.

panels.visual_description: Dutch Tilt, Close-up. Ned Land's face, frozen in confusion. The background lines slant, disrupting the stable 'botanical' order.
Close-up. Aronnax looks terrified, his eyes wide. The color palette shifts slightly to colder blues. The 'Botanical' precision makes his fear look frozen and statue-like.
panels.dialogue: Did I say sharks? I meant pearls. Sharks would not be sense.

panels.visual_description: Long Shot. The group settles back down, but the tension remains. Aronnax wipes his brow again. In the background, through the glass, a faint silhouette of a shark passes by, drawn with faint, ghostly ink lines.
panels.dialogue: Certainly not. But tell us now, how do they extract these pearls?
